pr.title: feat: Goodreads integration, Readwise↔Notion pipeline, reading status & ratings
pr.body:
## Summary

Adds Goodreads integration, enhances Readwise and Notion to support bidirectional sync, and introduces a Readwise→Notion highlight pipeline. Also adds reading status tracking and 5-star ratings across the platform.

**New Features:**
- **Goodreads integration** — Import your full library via CSV export or RSS feed sync. Imports shelves, ratings, reading status, reviews, and page counts.
- **Readwise↔Notion pipeline** — One-click flow that pulls all Readwise highlights and syncs them to Notion as organized book pages with quote blocks, notes, and tags.
- **Readwise bidirectional sync** — Push highlights TO Readwise and pull highlights FROM Readwise into BookForge.
- **Enhanced Notion sync** — Rich database properties: Status (select), Rating (number), Pages, Date Read, Highlights count, Source.
- **Reading status tracking** — To Read, Currently Reading, Finished, Abandoned — synced across all integrations.
- **5-star rating system** — Rate books, synced to Goodreads data and Notion.
- **Pipeline UI** — Visual pipeline cards on the Integrations dashboard showing Readwise→Notion and Readwise→BookForge flows.
- **Goodreads import modal** — Drag-drop CSV upload and RSS feed import with import statistics.

**Technical:**
- New `lib/integrations/goodreads.js` module (CSV parsing, RSS feed fetching, shelf mapping)
- Enhanced `readwise.js` with paginated `getHighlights()`, `getBooksWithHighlights()`, `importHighlights()`
- Enhanced `notion.js` with `syncReadwiseHighlights()`, `getPages()`, rich property support, block appending
- New `readwiseToNotion()` orchestrator in integrations index
- Database migrations for `reading_status`, `my_rating`, `goodreads_id`, `pages`, `date_read`, `review` columns
- New API endpoints: `/api/integrations/goodreads/import-csv`, `/api/integrations/goodreads/import-rss`, `/api/integrations/goodreads/import-all`, `/api/integrations/readwise/import`, `/api/integrations/readwise-to-notion`, `/api/books/:id/status`, `/api/books/:id/rating`, `/api/books/stats`

## Review & Testing Checklist for Human

- [ ] Test Goodreads CSV import: export from [goodreads.com/review/import](https://www.goodreads.com/review/import), upload via Integrations → Goodreads → Import Library. Verify books appear with correct status, rating, and tags.
- [ ] Test Readwise→Notion pipeline: configure `READWISE_ACCESS_TOKEN`, `NOTION_API_KEY`, and `NOTION_DATABASE_ID` in `.env`, then click "Readwise → Notion" pipeline card. Verify Notion pages are created with highlights.
- [ ] Test reading status and rating: open a book detail, click status buttons and star ratings. Verify they persist on page reload.
- [ ] Test Goodreads RSS import: set `GOODREADS_USER_ID` in `.env` (or enter in the RSS tab), import a shelf. Works best with public Goodreads profiles.

### Notes
- Goodreads shut down their official API in Dec 2020. This integration uses CSV export (most reliable) and RSS feeds (auto-sync). No OAuth needed.
- The Readwise→Notion pipeline requires both Readwise and Notion to be configured. It pulls all book highlights from Readwise and creates rich Notion pages.
- Database migrations run automatically — existing BookForge databases will get the new columns on first startup.
- `csv-parse` added as a new dependency for Goodreads CSV parsing.

Link to Devin session: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/2373b4bc39a943f6a0f0905f24b3c498
Requested by: @aalikes
pr.author: devin-ai-integration[bot]
